claim: Kimi K3 is competitive with at least some leading hosted or open-weight alternatives on at least one high-value workflow, without implying general frontier superiority.

searches_run: Kimi K3 vs GPT-5.5 Opus 4.8 cost performance
Artificial Analysis Kimi K3 Intelligence Index

grounds.content: On agentic tasks, K3 reaches an Elo rating of 1,668 on GDPval v2, a big jump from K2.6's 1,190. It beats GLM-5.2 (1,514), GPT-5.5 (1,494), and Claude Opus 4.8 (1,600), though it still falls short of Claude Fable 5 (1,760).
grounds.source_title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
grounds.locator_or_quote: On agentic tasks, K3 reaches an Elo rating of 1,668 on GDPval v2...
grounds.origin_path: Independent testing lab Artificial Analysis (cited by the-decoder.com)
limitations: GDPval v2 measures 'realistic, economically valuable tasks' but is a specific benchmark; results may not generalize to all high-value workflows.

grounds.content: K3 also takes the top spot on AutomationBench-AA, Artificial Analysis's version of Zapier's agentic SaaS workflow evaluation, with a score of 53 percent.
grounds.source_title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
grounds.locator_or_quote: K3 also takes the top spot on AutomationBench-AA... with a score of 53 percent.
grounds.origin_path: Independent testing lab Artificial Analysis (cited by the-decoder.com)
limitations: AutomationBench-AA is a specific evaluation of SaaS workflow automation; performance on other automation types may vary.

grounds.content: On AA-Briefcase, a private long-horizon knowledge work evaluation, K3 reaches an overall Elo of 1,547, up 732 points from K2.6. Only Claude Fable 5 scores higher.
grounds.source_title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
grounds.locator_or_quote: On AA-Briefcase... K3 reaches an overall Elo of 1,547... Only Claude Fable 5 scores higher.
grounds.origin_path: Independent testing lab Artificial Analysis (cited by the-decoder.com)
limitations: AA-Briefcase is a private, long-horizon knowledge work evaluation; details of tasks and scoring rubric are not public.

grounds.content: K3 lands around $0.94 per task, about level with GPT-5.5’s $0.99... and roughly half of Opus 4.8’s $1.80... The clean takeaway: K3 is materially cheaper than Opus 4.8 for comparable intelligence, cheaper per token than the GPT-5.x flagships, and roughly tied with GPT-5.5 once token usage is counted.
grounds.source_title: Kimi K3 vs GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.8 (2026): The Cheaper Peer
grounds.locator_or_quote: K3 lands around $0.94 per task... The clean takeaway...
grounds.origin_path: Analysis based on Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 cost data (cited by ofox.ai)
limitations: Cost per task is an aggregate measure from the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, which combines nine evaluations; cost-effectiveness may vary by specific workflow.

grounds.content: Kimi K3 scores 57 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, placing fourth overall behind Claude Fable 5 (60), GPT-5.6 Sol (59), and Claude Opus 4.8 (56).
grounds.source_title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
grounds.locator_or_quote: Kimi K3 scores 57 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, placing fourth overall...
grounds.origin_path: Independent testing lab Artificial Analysis (cited by the-decoder.com)
limitations: The Intelligence Index is a composite score; performance on constituent evaluations (e.g., coding, reasoning) varies. K3's score is very close to Opus 4.8's (56), indicating comparable overall performance in this composite measure.

grounds.content: Across all 35 tests, K3 took first place about seven times and landed second or third in most of the rest... In nearly every benchmark, K3 beat Opus 4.8, GPT 5.5, and GLM 5.2 by a wide margin.
grounds.source_title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
grounds.locator_or_quote: Across all 35 tests, K3 took first place about seven times...
grounds.origin_path: Independent testing lab Artificial Analysis (cited by the-decoder.com)
limitations: The set of 35 tests is not specified. Results 'weren't all collected under identical conditions' as different agent systems (KimiCode, Claude Code, Codex) were used depending on the benchmark.

inaccessible_or_missing: Primary source report from Artificial Analysis lab for the K3 evaluation.
Primary source technical report or benchmark details from Moonshot AI for K3.
lanes.coverage_notes: Grounds are derived from syndicated reports of independent evaluations by Artificial Analysis, which operationalizes performance via specific benchmarks (GDPval v2, AutomationBench-AA, AA-Briefcase, Intelligence Index) relevant to high-value workflows like agentic tasks, SaaS automation, and long-horizon knowledge work. Cost-effectiveness data is also provided. The sources consistently show K3 outperforming models like GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.8 on several key measures, supporting competitiveness without claiming general frontier superiority (as it trails Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol).

searches_run: Kimi K3 vs Claude Fable 5 benchmark
Kimi K3 vs Fable 5 cost speed comparison

grounds.content: In a direct comparison on three coding tasks (a bug fix, a multi-file refactor, and a feature build), Kimi K3 produced identical or correct results compared to Anthropic's Fable 5. For the bug fix, both models produced a byte-for-byte identical diff that passed all tests. For the refactor, both models passed all 264 tests. For the feature build, both delivered a correct, working feature.
grounds.source_title: Claude Fable 5 vs. Kimi K3: Same results, one-third the cost, 4x slower - The New Stack
grounds.locator_or_quote:
The metric | Kimi K3 | Fable 5
--- | --- | ---
Bug fix | identical fix, 70/70 tests | identical fix, 70/70 tests
Refactor | 264/264 tests | 264/264 tests
Feature | correct, 6 files | correct, 7 files
limitations: The test was conducted on a small set of three specific coding tasks.
The tests were run using different native CLI tools (Kimi Code for K3, Claude Code for Fable 5), which introduces a variable beyond just the models themselves.

grounds.content: While Kimi K3 achieved the same correct outcomes as Fable 5 on three coding tasks, it was significantly slower. The total time for Kimi K3 was 28 minutes and 18 seconds, compared to 6 minutes and 49 seconds for Fable 5, making it roughly four times slower overall. The author noted this was "by far the longest time I’ve seen" in their weekly tests.
grounds.source_title: Claude Fable 5 vs. Kimi K3: Same results, one-third the cost, 4x slower - The New Stack
grounds.locator_or_quote: Fable finished all three jobs in under 7 minutes. Kimi needed just over 28. I run different tests on models weekly, and this was by far the longest time I’ve seen.
limitations: Latency was measured using a stopwatch, which is less precise than automated logging.
The performance was measured via specific CLI tools, which could contribute to the time difference.

grounds.content: Independent testing by Artificial Analysis places Kimi K3's intelligence on par with leading models like GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.8, though still behind Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol. On the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, K3 scored 57, behind Fable 5 (60) and GPT-5.6 Sol (59).
grounds.source_title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
grounds.locator_or_quote: Independent testing lab Artificial Analysis has published its first evaluation of Kimi K3. The model scores 57 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, putting it on par with Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 but still behind Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol.
limitations: The 'Intelligence Index' is an aggregate score, and may not reflect performance on a specific high-value workflow.
The article notes that for the underlying benchmarks, different agent systems were used for different models, meaning results were not all collected under identical conditions.

grounds.content: On agentic workflow evaluations, Kimi K3 shows competitive performance. It achieved a top score of 53% on AutomationBench-AA (an agentic SaaS workflow evaluation) and an Elo of 1,668 on GDPval v2, surpassing Opus 4.8 (1,600) but not Fable 5 (1,760). On the AA-Briefcase long-horizon knowledge work evaluation, only Fable 5 scored higher than K3.
grounds.source_title: Kimi's open model K3 nears GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 while signaling the end of super cheap Chinese AI
grounds.locator_or_quote: On agentic tasks, K3 reaches an Elo rating of 1,668 on GDPval v2, a big jump from K2.6's 1,190. It beats GLM-5.2 (1,514), GPT-5.5 (1,494), and Claude Opus 4.8 (1,600), though it still falls short of Claude Fable 5 (1,760). K3 also takes the top spot on AutomationBench-AA, Artificial Analysis's version of Zapier's agentic SaaS workflow evaluation, with a score of 53 percent.
